provenance.source_corpus: Experimental reintroduction of the crayfish species Orconectes virilis into formerly acidified Lake 302S (Experimental Lakes Area, Canada)This paper is part of the series “Forty Years of Aquatic Research at the Experimental Lakes Area”.

nodes.name: total biomass of other benthic invertebrates

source_spans.text: Orconectes virilis significantly (P < 0.05) suppressed the total biomass of other benthic invertebrates by 70%
crayfish reduced periphytic biomass by 90%

nodes.name: biomass of the most abundant odonate, Aeshna sp.

source_spans.text: Orconectes virilis enclosures contained significantly less biomass of the most abundant odonate, Aeshna sp.
crayfish significantly increased the population density of this anisopteran
crayfish significantly increased total zygopteran biomass
O. virilis reduced chlorophyll concentrations
suppressing filamentous green algae
crayfish significantly decreased benthic diatoms
O. virilis significantly suppressed chironomids
crayfish significantly suppressed leeches

edges.original_sentence: Orconectes virilis significantly (P < 0.05) suppressed the total biomass of other benthic invertebrates by 70% primarily because of declines in larval damselflies and midges.

edges.original_sentence: Similarly, competitive interference could also account for why crayfish significantly suppressed leeches, which occupied a similar trophic position as O. virilis based on stable isotopic evidence.
